### Post by McAngus on 2008-10-24
installed nvidia GeForce 8500 GT

installed latest driver from nvidia: ver. 177.80

downloaded nvidia-xconfig-1.0 files from nvidia,but don't know what to do with them or if I need them

modified my xorg.conf, this is what it looks like:

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder63)  Wed Oct  1 15:09:35 PDT 2008

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0"
    Screen      1  "Screen1" leftOf "Screen0"
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Main Monitor"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 110.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 150.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Second Monitor"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BusID          "PCI:3:0:0"
    Screen         0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BusID          "PCI:3:0:0"
    Screen         1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Main Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen1"
    Device         "Device1"
    Monitor        "Main Monitor"
    Option         "TVStandard" "NTSC-M"
    Option         "UseDisplayDevice" "TV-0"
    Option         "ConnectedMonitor" "Second Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes       "1024x768_60"
    EndSubSection
EndSection
```

located Xorg.0.log error, it reads:

```
(WW) NVIDIA(1): Unable to find any of the requested display device "TV-0" in
(WW) NVIDIA(1):     the list of available display devices "".
(EE) NVIDIA(1): Unable to find available Display Devices for screen 1.
```

it's taken a long time to get here, and now I'm delirious, please help, monitor 1 runs fine, **just can't get the card to use the tv-out to send screen to the projector.**

Specs:
Ubuntu 8.04 hardy
Compiz cube enabled
GeForce 8500 GT
Monitor 1 - Samsung SyncMaster 226bw hooked up via DVI
Monitor 2 - Optoma projector hooked up via Svideo dongle to composite to DVI:confused:

### Post by doas777 on 2008-10-24
under screen1, do you mean to have 
```
 Monitor        "Main Monitor"

``` ?

it seems like it shoudl be 
```
 Monitor        "Second Monitor"
``` 

I also usually have to set the HSynch and VRefresh for my tvs in the monitor section. 
HTH
good luck,
Franklin

---

### Post by McAngus on 2008-10-25
Thank You Very Much for your response,

changed  Monitor to "Second Monitor" under Screen1
added HSynch and VRefresh to the tv's Monitor section

Same Error.

Why does it not know what TV-0 is?

Where is this "list of available display devices" it refers to?

Can that list be amended to include TV-0?

Does my projector need to be on and the source locked to DVI during restart for xorg.conf to recognize it? (it has been, but if it doesn't need to be, I would like to leave the projector off until the problem has been solved)

Could it be my BusID for Device1? (I know nothing about bus addresses) (card works for my monitor, could the BusID be slightly different for the TV-out?)


if you can't tell, I REALLY don't know what I'm doing, everything I've learned has been trial and error for the last 5 days.
